title: cuphea rupestris
intro: Cuphea rupestris, also known as Rock Cuphea, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and parts of Central America, characterized by its bright yellow, orange, or red flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Cuphea rupestris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although proper dosages and preparation methods should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
